findings.summary: MAX20405AFOF_VY+T (synchronous buck converter) classified as LDO in dcdc.kicad_sch

correct.description: IC3 (MAX20405) correctly identified as a power regulator with +5V output

incorrect.description: MAX20405 classified with topology=LDO but is actually a synchronous buck converter. Has inductor L1 + bootstrap cap C15 which are hallmarks of switching topology.

suggestions: Check for inductor on output (buck indicator), BST/BOOT pins (bootstrap = switching), and IC keywords like buck/switching/step-down

findings.summary: Bootstrap cap C15 between BST and LX pins on MAX20405 falsely detected as LC filter with L1

incorrect.description: L1 (4.7uH) + C15 (0.1uF) flagged as LC filter at 232 kHz resonance. C15 is a bootstrap capacitor connected between BST and LX (switching node), not part of a filter.

suggestions: Recognize BST/BOOT pin names and exclude bootstrap cap circuits from LC filter detection

findings.summary: pwr_flag_warnings generated for sub-sheets where power rail source is on a different sheet (29 warnings across OpenMower, 37 in cynthion, 9 in education_tools, 6 in glasgow)

correct.description: Technically correct: each sub-sheet individually lacks power_out or PWR_FLAG on those rails

incorrect.description: In hierarchical designs, power rails sourced on one sheet appear as power_in-only on other sheets. Warnings are noisy since the PWR_FLAG/power_out exists in the hierarchy, just not on this particular sheet.

suggestions: Cross-reference hierarchically: if a power net has power_out/PWR_FLAG on ANY sheet in the design, suppress the warning on other sheets. Or mark these as informational rather than warnings.

findings.summary: OpenMower uses property name "Digikey" (capital D, lowercase k) with 200 occurrences. get_property() does exact case match so "DigiKey" in the fallback chain misses "Digikey".

missed.description: digikey field is empty for all OpenMower components despite source having "Digikey" property with valid PNs (e.g. "1276-1935-1-ND")

suggestions: Add "Digikey" (exact case) to the fallback chain in analyze_schematic.py line 372-376, or make get_property() case-insensitive for distributor fields

findings.summary: R5/R4 ratio=0.985 on RASPI_FAN_ON detected as voltage divider — this is a pull-up/pull-down on a digital GPIO signal, not a sensing divider

incorrect.description: R5/R4 with ratio=0.985 on RASPI_FAN_ON. The near-unity ratio and digital signal name indicate pull-up/pull-down, not a voltage divider for sensing.

suggestions: Ratios very close to 0 or 1 (e.g. >0.95) often indicate pull-up/pull-down pairs, not dividers

findings.summary: Unused opamp units 3 and 4 of U2 (MCP604) incorrectly assigned input_resistor R49

correct.description: U2 units 3 and 4 correctly identified as comparator_or_open_loop with both inputs at GND

incorrect.description: Units 3 and 4 have input_resistor={ref:R49, ohms:4700}. R49 is part of the R48/R49 voltage divider feeding unit 2's inverting input. The analyzer erroneously associates R49 as an input resistor for these unused units because R49 has one terminal on GND, which is the same net as these units' inputs.

suggestions: When an opamp's inputs are tied directly to GND/VCC (unused unit), do not search for input resistors on those power nets

findings.summary: R11/R12 feedback network for MAX20405 buck converter classified as voltage_divider but missed as feedback_network

correct.description: R11 (53k6) / R12 (10k) correctly identified as a voltage divider with ratio 0.157 from +5V to GND, midpoint to JP5

missed.description: R11/R12 form the output voltage feedback divider for IC3 (MAX20405 buck converter). The midpoint connects through JP5 to the FB pin of IC3. This should be detected as a feedback_network in addition to being a voltage_divider.

suggestions: When a voltage divider's midpoint connects to a regulator IC's feedback pin (FB, VSEN, ADJ), also classify it as a feedback_network

findings.summary: BSS138 level shifters Q13-Q16 not recognized as level_shifter topology

correct.description: Q13-Q16 correctly identified as NMOS transistor circuits with gate=+3V3, drain=SIGNAL_HV, source=SIGNAL_LV

missed.description: Q13-Q16 (BSS138) are classic MOSFET bidirectional level shifters (AN10441 topology). Gate tied to lower voltage rail (+3V3), drain on HV side with pull-up, source on LV side with pull-up. The analyzer detects them as transistor circuits but does not recognize the level-shifter pattern. No level_shifter signal path category exists.

suggestions: Add level_shifter detection: NMOS with gate=power_rail, source on one signal domain, drain on another, with pull-up resistors on both sides

findings.summary: Q13-Q16 transistor gate_resistors list includes unrelated resistors from the +3V3 net

incorrect.description: Q13-Q16 each show gate_resistors including R48, R14, R17, R19, R15, R3, R9. Only R3 (for Q13) or the local pull-up is the actual gate-related resistor. The others are on the +3V3 power rail net and have no functional relationship to the gate drive of these MOSFETs.

suggestions: When the gate net is a power rail, do not enumerate all resistors on that rail as gate_resistors. Only include resistors directly in the gate drive path.
